items.front: When should the word "takeoff" be used in ATC phraseology?
items.back: Use it only when actually clearing an aircraft for takeoff or canceling a takeoff clearance.

items.front: What must an IFR departure clearance include?
items.back: Include the clearance limit, needed departure procedure or heading, route, and altitude using the paragraph's SID, ODP, and expectation rules.

items.front: When may an abbreviated departure clearance be used?
items.back: Only when the filed route has not changed, all facilities have enough route information, the destination is relayed when required, and the assigned altitude is stated.

items.front: What tools may ATC use to control IFR departure timing from non-towered airports?
items.back: Use a departure release, hold for release, release time, departure restriction, or clearance void time as needed to separate or regulate departures.

items.front: What if a ground stop applies to the aircraft?
items.back: Do not release the aircraft without approval from the originator of the ground stop.

items.front: How should ground delays requested by aircraft be sequenced?
items.back: If practicable, issue departure clearances in the order the original clearance requests were made.

items.front: Who must be advised of anticipated departure delays?
items.back: Inform the affected approach control facilities and or towers.

items.front: When is coordination with the receiving facility required before departure?
items.back: Coordinate before departure if the point of departure is within the required flying-time limit of the boundary, unless agreed automation transfer procedures replace it.

items.front: How is a VFR release of an IFR departure handled?
items.back: After needed approval, you may authorize a VFR departure and tell the pilot when or where to obtain the IFR clearance; if IFR cannot be issued, suggest taking the delay on the ground.

items.front: When must terminal departure times be forwarded?
items.back: Unless an LOA or automatic message system replaces it, forward the departure time to the facility that issued the clearance and to terminal departure control when involved.
